tools.name: CRAFT - the perfect prompt
tools.primaryUseCase: C.R.A.F.T. stands for Character, Relevance, Ask, Format, Tone — a simple method to create powerful AI prompts that actually get the job done.

tools.name: Newton - General SGM Helper
tools.primaryUseCase: A GPT that has lots of different information about a range of SGM related topic. This is a direct copy of the one provided to clients.
tools.description: This GPT serves as a comprehensive knowledge repository covering a wide range of SGM-related topics, providing users with the exact same information and resources as the version shared with clients. It is designed to offer detailed insights and support across all relevant subject areas within the SGM framework to ensure consistency and depth.
tools.howItWorks:
It is filled with company wide inforation from most departments and allows the user to ask questions against it. 

Its limitations are that it could be out of date as it will require the owner to mannually upload new knowledge files.
tools.techUsed: ChatGPT only. There are no external calls

tools.name: Single GEO Audit
tools.primaryUseCase: Carries out a single GEO audit on a given website URL will currently produce a SGM branded PDF
tools.description: An automated AI-search visibility audit. Give it any website URL and it scores how well that site is set up to be found and cited by AI-powered search — Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity and Bing Copilot — then returns a branded Sleeping Giant Media PDF report and writes the results to a Google Sheet. It replaces a manual GEO audit that used to take hours with a consistent, repeatable score in under a minute.
tools.howItWorks:
A website URL is submitted to the audit API (triggered from n8n, a form, or a direct request).
The tool crawls the site — homepage, key pages, robots.txt and sitemap — fetching directly first and falling back to a cloud crawler for bot-protected sites.
It scores the site deterministically across 10 weighted GEO categories (crawlability, citability, schema, authority, AI-crawler access, and more). No AI/LLM is used in the scoring — the same site always gets the same score.
From those scores it produces an overall 0–100 rating, four platform sub-scores, a verdict, and prioritised findings and recommendations.
The results are rendered into a branded SGM PDF and, if a Sheet is supplied, written into a Google Sheet for tracking.
tools.techUsed:
Railway — hosts the service; deploys automatically on every push to GitHub.
GitHub — source code; the single source of truth for what's live.
n8n — orchestrates the workflow: triggers audits and routes the PDF onward to email/Drive.
Apify — fallback web crawler for sites behind CDN/bot protection.
Google Sheets — audit results written to a tracking sheet via a service account.
Playwright / Chromium — renders the HTML report into a print-quality A4 PDF.
Premier League "Twin Audit" (new) — a campaign variant running the same engine, benchmarking a prospect against the 20 PL clubs.

tools.name: SGM Presentation Designer
tools.primaryUseCase: Take a presentation idea or thought presentation plan and turn it into an SGM-branded visual presentation that can be exported to Google Slides
tools.description: An automated tool that will take design or presentation concepts and turn them into a designed SGM branded deck. Due to the nature of LLMs, the deck design is not consistent every time, so this may not be viable for external presentations. However, for internal presentations, it generally produces a great outcome
tools.howItWorks: It works by taking the presentation idea or full contextual overview and running it through a detailed prompt that pulls on key SGM design elements to produce an output that is visualised within Gemini and can be exported to Google Slides. Albeit, once exported to Google Slides, the design element can change, but it is aimed to get the presentation to 80 or 90% of the way complete

tools.name: Single Content Audit
tools.primaryUseCase: This is a mini content audit tool that will analyse five to ten pages on a client's website and extract insights and information on the website to give a seven-page PDF output, which will be perfect for lead gen conversations
tools.description: An AI-powered audit tool that scans any website and produces a Sleeping Giant Media branded PDF report plus a structured Google Sheet row of the data. It scores the site against Google's E-E-A-T framework (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness) — the same signals Google and AI engines like ChatGPT and Perplexity use to decide what content to cite. We use it for prospect lead-gen, new client onboarding, discovery work, and identifying upsell opportunities across SEO, content, and (soon) Digital PR services.
tools.howItWorks:
You give it a URL. It then runs a fully automated pipeline:

Site map scan — pulls the site's sitemap to understand size and freshness
Page selection — auto-picks 5–10 representative pages (home, about, services, case studies, blog) or accepts a manual list
Page crawl + screenshots — renders each page in a real browser, capturing HTML, copy, structured data, and an above-the-fold screenshot
On-page analysis — extracts headings, schema markup, image alt-text coverage, internal/external links, and readability metrics
Off-page entity check — looks up the brand in Google's Knowledge Graph, Wikidata, and runs a branded Google search to see how the brand shows up in the SERP (review sites, social profiles, knowledge panel)
AI scoring — sends all the collected evidence to Claude (Anthropic's AI model), which scores the site against the E-E-A-T framework, identifies critical/high/medium/low issues, recommends actions, observes tone of voice, and flags upsell opportunities
Output — assembles the findings into a 7-slide branded PDF and writes the structured data into the Google Sheet
End-to-end run time: ~2–4 minutes per audit. Cost per run: ~£0.10–0.25.
tools.techUsed:
FastAPI (Python) — the web service that receives the audit request and runs the pipeline
Apify — cloud web scraping. Bypasses bot-blocking CDNs and renders pages with a real browser. Three actors: content crawler, screenshot, and Google SERP scraper
Anthropic Claude (Sonnet 4.6) — the AI model that does the qualitative scoring and writes the findings. OpenAI GPT-4o as a fallback
Google Knowledge Graph API + Wikidata API — off-site entity verification (does Google "know" this brand as a real entity?)
Playwright — headless browser used to render the final HTML report into a print-ready PDF
Jinja2 — templating engine that builds the branded HTML report from the audit data
textstat — Python library for objective readability metrics (Flesch–Kincaid grade, sentence length)
httpx — async HTTP client used for the broken-link checker
Google Sheets API — writes the structured audit data into a shared spreadsheet for the team to filter and track
Railway — cloud hosting. Auto-deploys whenever code is pushed to GitHub
GitHub — source-code version control. The repo is lukequilt123/sgm-content-audit-api
n8n — the workflow tool the team uses to trigger audits and route the PDF/Sheet output
